input.command:
gh issue create --repo TheurgicDuke771/DataQ --title "admin webhook-config surface mislabels dbt connections as airflow (two-provider if/else missed by ADR 0029)" --label bug --milestone "v1.1 Week 2 — Portability: OTel logs, secrets lifecycle, dry-run depth" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

`admin_service.webhook_configs()` loops over `ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS` (now `("adf", "airflow", "dbt")` since #611/ADR 0029) but its body still branches `if provider == "adf": … else: # airflow`. A `dbt` connection falls into the else-branch and is emitted with:

- `provider="airflow"` (admin_service.py:231)
- `inbound_url=…/api/v1/orchestration/events/airflow` (:233) — wrong endpoint
- `signing_secret_name=settings.airflow_webhook_secret_name` (:235) — wrong secret name

The frontend admin page (`frontend/src/api/admin.ts:72` → `GET /admin/orchestration/webhooks`) surfaces these rows directly, so an admin configuring a dbt webhook gets guidance pointing at the Airflow endpoint and the Airflow signing key.

## Root cause

`webhook_configs` was written in #490/#493 when the tuple was two providers; the #611 migration widened the tuple + routes but didn't touch this function. `backend/tests/api/test_admin.py` covers adf and airflow rows but never creates a `dbt` connection, so nothing caught the fallthrough.

## Related gap (same fix PR can cover it)

`.env.app.example` documents `ADF_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME` / `AIRFLOW_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME` but not `DBT_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME` (config.py has the Python default `dbt-webhook-secret`, but the env template should list the knob like its siblings).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `webhook_configs()` dispatches per provider (no adf-vs-else fallthrough) — dbt rows carry `provider="dbt"`, the `/orchestration/events/dbt` inbound URL, and `settings.dbt_webhook_secret_name`
- [ ] Test creates a `dbt` connection and asserts its webhook-config row (and guards the next provider addition — parametrize over `ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS`)
- [ ] `DBT_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME` documented in `.env.app.example`

Found by the /code-review pass on #646 (cross-file tracer angle) and verified against source + git history.
EOF
)"
input.description: File issue for admin_service dbt mislabel bug

result.output: https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/647

input.description: List harness repo and grep script for dbt

result.output:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# harness_window.sh — open/close an ad-hoc DataQ harness test window on Azure.
3	#
4	# The harness compute was stopped 2026-07-04 (cost wind-down, DataQ #590): while
5	# awake it burns ~CAD 17/day (~0.70/hour); stopped it costs ~0. This script wakes
6	# it, optionally kicks the data flows, and puts it back to sleep.
7	#
8	# Usage:
9	#   harness_window.sh status                  # show app/trigger/job states (read-only)
10	#   harness_window.sh start                    # wake: redis -> airflow -> worker -> trigger, ADF triggers on
11	#   harness_window.sh run [--adf] [--dags]     # resume+kick the 5 mockdata jobs as MANUAL executions, re-suspend after
12	#                                              #   --adf:  manual create-run of the Flow-A ADF pipelines*
13	#                                              #   --dags: manual-trigger the 3 cron DAGs via the Airflow REST API
14	#                                              #     (POST /api/v1/dags/{id}/dagRuns, basic auth; unpauses first --
15	#                                              #      a run created on a paused DAG never executes). Needs the
16	#                                              #      harness awake. flow_a_payments_event is NOT force-triggered:
17	#                                              #      it requires an Event Grid conf payload and fires naturally
18	#                                              #      when the mockdata files land while the harness is awake.
19	#                                              #      flow_a_snowflake_load fails after the Snowflake lapse (#588).
20	#   harness_window.sh stop                     # sleep: triggers off, jobs suspended, apps stopped (reverse order)
21	#   harness_window.sh window [--adf] [--dags]  # start -> run -> stop, one shot
22	#
23	# Job semantics: a suspended ACA job REFUSES manual starts, so `run` resumes each
24	# job, starts the execution, and suspends again at the end — everything executes
25	# on demand, never waiting for (or leaving armed) the cron schedules. `run` works
26	# standalone (mockdata jobs have no dependency on the Airflow apps).
27	#
28	# *--adf create-runs pl_flow_a_customers + pl_flow_a_orders. Flow A loads
29	#  SNOWFLAKE — after the Snowflake subscription lapses (v1.1 W1, #588) these runs
30	#  will fail; the mockdata jobs (file landing) keep working regardless.
31	#  Airflow DAGs are NOT triggered here — they fire on their own cron while the
32	#  scheduler is awake (or trigger manually in the Airflow UI).
33	set -euo pipefail
34	
35	SUB="37e710fd-b956-4a2e-94f2-b132bb26b3d6"
36	RG="dataq-rg"
37	ADF="dataq-harness-adf"
38	ARM="https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUB/resourceGroups/$RG/providers"
39	APP_API="2024-03-01"
40	JOB_API="2024-08-02-preview"
41	AIRFLOW_URL="https://dataq-harness-airflow.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io"
42	TF_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../terraform" && pwd)"   # harness TF holds the Airflow admin creds
43	DAGS=(flow_a_snowflake_load flow_a_uc_reference flow_b_medallion)     # cron DAGs; payments_event is event-only
44	
45	# Wake order matters: broker first, scheduler before workers. Stop is the reverse.
46	APPS_START=(dataq-harness-redis dataq-harness-airflow dataq-harness-airflow-worker dataq-harness-airflow-trigger)
47	APPS_STOP=(dataq-harness-airflow-trigger dataq-harness-airflow-worker dataq-harness-airflow dataq-harness-redis)
48	TRIGGERS=(tr_customers_daily tr_orders_landed)
49	JOBS=(mockdata-orders mockdata-inventory mockdata-tracking mockdata-feedback mockdata-supply)
50	
51	log() { printf '%s %s\n' "$(date +%H:%M:%S)" "$*"; }
52	
53	app_state() { az containerapp show -n "$1" -g "$RG" --query properties.runningStatus -o tsv 2>/dev/null; }
54	
55	wait_app() { # wait_app <name> <wanted-state>
56	  local name=$1 want=$2 i
57	  for i in $(seq 1 60); do
58	    [[ "$(app_state "$name")" == "$want" ]] && { log "  $name -> $want"; return 0; }
59	    sleep 5
60	  done
61	  log "  TIMEOUT: $name never reached $want"; return 1
62	}
63	
64	cmd_status() {
65	  az containerapp list -g "$RG" --query "[?starts_with(name,'dataq-harness')].{name:name, running:properties.runningStatus}" -o table
66	  az datafactory trigger list -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --query "[].{name:name, state:properties.runtimeState}" -o table
67	  local j
68	  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
69	    printf '%s last execution: ' "$j"
70	    az rest --method GET --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/executions?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null |
71	      python3 -c "import json,sys; v=json.load(sys.stdin)['value']; print(v[0]['properties']['status']+' @ '+v[0]['properties']['startTime'] if v else 'none')"
72	  done
73	}
74	
75	cmd_start() {
76	  local app t j
77	  log "Waking harness (billing ~CAD 0.70/hour while awake)..."
78	  for app in "${APPS_START[@]}"; do
79	    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/containerApps/$app/start?api-version=$APP_API" -o none 2>/dev/null || true
80	    wait_app "$app" Running
81	    if [[ "$app" == "dataq-harness-airflow" ]]; then
82	      log "  waiting for Airflow /health..."
83	      for _ in $(seq 1 60); do
84	        curl -sf --max-time 10 "$AIRFLOW_URL/health" >/dev/null 2>&1 && { log "  Airflow healthy"; break; }
85	        sleep 10
86	      done
87	    fi
88	  done
89	  for t in "${TRIGGERS[@]}"; do
90	    az datafactory trigger start -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --name "$t" >/dev/null && log "  ADF trigger $t -> Started"
91	  done
92	  log "Harness is UP (mockdata crons stay suspended; 'run' fires them manually). Remember to '$0 stop' when done."
93	}
94	
95	cmd_dags() {
96	  local user pass d rid state
97	  user=$(terraform -chdir="$TF_DIR" output -raw airflow_admin_user)
98	  pass=$(terraform -chdir="$TF_DIR" output -raw airflow_admin_password)
99	  log "Triggering the cron DAGs via the Airflow REST API (sequential)..."
100	  for d in "${DAGS[@]}"; do
101	    # a dagRun created on a paused DAG sits queued forever -> unpause first (idempotent)
102	    curl -sf --max-time 30 -X PATCH "$AIRFLOW_URL/api/v1/dags/$d?update_mask=is_paused" \
103	      -H 'Content-Type: application/json' --user "$user:$pass" -d '{"is_paused": false}' >/dev/null
104	    rid=$(curl -sf --max-time 30 -X POST "$AIRFLOW_URL/api/v1/dags/$d/dagRuns" \
105	      -H 'Content-Type: application/json' --user "$user:$pass" -d '{}' |
106	      python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['dag_run_id'])")
107	    log "  $d -> $rid"
108	    for _ in $(seq 1 60); do
109	      state=$(curl -sf --max-time 30 --user "$user:$pass" \
110	        "$AIRFLOW_URL/api/v1/dags/$d/dagRuns/$rid" |
111	        python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['state'])" 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)
112	      [[ "$state" != "queued" && "$state" != "running" && "$state" != "unknown" ]] && break
113	      sleep 15
114	    done
115	    log "  $d: $state"
116	  done
117	}
118	
119	cmd_run() {
120	  local with_adf="" with_dags="" a j execs=()

1	# dbt Core lineage project (`dataq_lineage`) — harness #609
2	
3	Self-hosted **dbt Core** project that builds multi-hop lineage objects on top of the
4	Flow-A retail base tables in Snowflake:
5	
6	```
7	DATAQ_DB.RETAIL.{ORDERS_HEADER, ORDER_LINES, CUSTOMERS, PRODUCTS}   ← base (Airflow/ADF loads)
8	        │  (source)
9	        ▼
10	DATAQ_DB.ANALYTICS_STG.{stg_orders, stg_order_lines, stg_customers, stg_products}   ← staging VIEWS
11	        │  (ref)
12	        ▼
13	DATAQ_DB.ANALYTICS.{mart_order_revenue, mart_customer_orders}   ← mart DYNAMIC TABLES (target_lag 1h)
14	```
15	
16	It exists to (a) exercise **dbt as a third orchestration layer** (the DataQ-side
17	provider is a separate issue, #611) and (b) be the **test bed for the G-d lineage
18	work** (#596 — `manifest.json` is the node-graph input). Harness-only, ADR 0021 —
19	**not** DataQ app code, **not** git-tracked.
20	
21	## Why this shape
22	- **Staging = views, marts = dynamic tables** → real base→staging→mart lineage, and
23	  the dynamic tables' refresh lag gives DataQ a **freshness monitor** to measure.
24	- **Self-hosted dbt Core**, host-agnostic on purpose: dbt Cloud free has no
25	  API/scheduler, dbt-on-Snowflake dies with the trial, Databricks-Jobs-dbt couples
26	  to the Jobs API. A plain container survives the cloud wind-down (#590/#591); the
27	  provider (#611) observes it purely through **artifacts + webhook**, never a
28	  host-specific API.
29	
30	## Authentication & privileges
31	- **dbt → Snowflake:** password auth, env-var driven (`profiles.yml` is 100%
32	  `env_var()`, no secret in the file). Same creds the Airflow loader uses.
33	- **Role = `DATAQ_LOADER`, not `ACCOUNTADMIN`.** dbt owns the two `ANALYTICS*`
34	  schemas (needs `CREATE SCHEMA ON DATABASE`, granted in `../terraform/snowflake.tf`
35	  → `loader_create_schema`) and grants `SELECT`/`USAGE` on them to `DATAQ_READER`
36	  (the on-run-end hooks) so a DataQ suite (running as the least-priv reader) can read
37	  the marts. Override with `DBT_SNOWFLAKE_ROLE` (defaults to `DATAQ_LOADER`; we use a
38	  dbt-specific var so `SNOWFLAKE_ROLE=ACCOUNTADMIN` in `secrets.sh` can't re-escalate).
39	- **Change tracking (dynamic-table prerequisite):** dynamic tables require change
40	  tracking on their base tables, and `DATAQ_LOADER` can't enable it (doesn't own
41	  them). `ORDERS_HEADER` + `CUSTOMERS` → `change_tracking = true` in `snowflake.tf`;
42	  `ORDER_LINES` + `PRODUCTS` (write_pandas-created) → run **`bootstrap_change_tracking.sql`**
43	  once as `ACCOUNTADMIN`. ⚠️ `write_pandas` overwrite `CREATE OR REPLACE`s the latter
44	  two and drops the setting — stable during the wind-down (loader stopped), re-run the
45	  bootstrap if `flow_a_snowflake_load` runs again.
46	- **dbt → ADLS (artifact upload):** `AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING` (same seam as
47	  mockdata's `AdlsStore`).
48	
49	## Hosting (one image, three homes)
50	| Context | How |
51	|---|---|
52	| Dev iteration | local venv/conda: `source ../secrets.sh && dbt build --profiles-dir .` |
53	| Azure window | ACA Job `dbt-lineage` on `dataq-cae` (`../terraform/dbt_job.tf`) |
54	| Post-wind-down | `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml run --rm dbt` |
55	
56	## Run it
57	
58	### Local (fastest dev loop — hits live Snowflake, not the Azure harness)
59	```bash
60	# one-time: change tracking on the write_pandas base tables (as ACCOUNTADMIN)
61	source ../secrets.sh
62	snowsql -a "$SNOWFLAKE_ACCOUNT" -u "$SNOWFLAKE_USER" -r ACCOUNTADMIN \
63	  -d DATAQ_DB -s RETAIL -f bootstrap_change_tracking.sql   # or paste into a worksheet
64	
65	# build (needs a local dbt-snowflake — see requirements.txt)
66	pip install -r requirements.txt
67	dbt deps            # no-op (no packages) but harmless
68	dbt build --profiles-dir .
69	# artifacts land in ./target/{manifest,run_results}.json
70	```
71	
72	### Container / ACA Job (the "runs from the container host" acceptance)
73	```bash
74	# build + push the image to the harness ACR (login server from terraform output)
75	az acr login -n <registry>
76	docker build -t <login_server>/dbt-lineage:latest .          # context = this dir
77	docker push <login_server>/dbt-lineage:latest
78	terraform -chdir=../terraform apply -target=azurerm_container_app_job.dbt_lineage
79	az containerapp job start -n dbt-lineage -g dataq-rg          # on-demand run
80	```
81	
82	## Artifacts (`#611` / `#596`)
83	`upload_artifacts.py` publishes `manifest.json` + `run_results.json` to
84	`adls://<landing-account>/raw/dbt/runs/<UTC-ts>/` and overwrites `.../raw/dbt/latest/`
85	(stable pointer for the #611 poller). No-op locally (`DBT_ARTIFACTS_URI` unset).
86	
87	## DataQ suite target
88	Point a DataQ Snowflake suite at `DATAQ_DB.ANALYTICS.MART_ORDER_REVENUE`
89	(checks on `line_count`/`reconciled_subtotal` + a **freshness** monitor on `ORDER_TS`)
90	to prove DataQ runs against non-base, dbt-produced dynamic tables.
91

120	  local with_adf="" with_dags="" a j execs=()
121	  for a in "$@"; do
122	    [[ "$a" == "--adf" ]] && with_adf=1
123	    [[ "$a" == "--dags" ]] && with_dags=1
124	  done
125	  log "Starting the 5 mockdata jobs as manual executions (parallel)..."
126	  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
127	    # a suspended job refuses manual starts -> resume first (idempotent), suspend again after the waits
128	    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/resume?api-version=$JOB_API" -o none 2>/dev/null || true
129	    local ename
130	    ename=$(az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/start?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('name',''))")
131	    log "  $j -> execution ${ename:-<unknown>}"
132	    execs+=("$j:$ename")
133	  done
134	  log "Waiting for job executions to finish (timeout 35m each)..."
135	  local pair status
136	  for pair in "${execs[@]}"; do
137	    j=${pair%%:*}; local e=${pair#*:}
138	    [[ -z "$e" ]] && continue
139	    for _ in $(seq 1 70); do
140	      status=$(az rest --method GET --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/executions/$e?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['properties']['status'])" 2>/dev/null || echo Unknown)
141	      [[ "$status" != "Running" && "$status" != "Unknown" ]] && break
142	      sleep 30
143	    done
144	    log "  $j: $status"
145	  done
146	  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
147	    # retry: suspend 409s while a start/resume operation is still provisioning
148	    for _ in $(seq 1 8); do
149	      az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/suspend?api-version=$JOB_API" -o none 2>/dev/null && break
150	      sleep 15
151	    done
152	  done
153	  log "  mockdata jobs re-suspended (crons disarmed)"
154	  [[ -n "$with_dags" ]] && cmd_dags
155	  if [[ -n "$with_adf" ]]; then
156	    log "Triggering Flow-A ADF pipelines (needs live Snowflake!)..."
157	    local p rid
158	    for p in pl_flow_a_customers pl_flow_a_orders; do
159	      rid=$(az datafactory pipeline create-run -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --name "$p" --query runId -o tsv)
160	      log "  $p -> run $rid"
161	      for _ in $(seq 1 60); do
162	        status=$(az datafactory pipeline-run show -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --run-id "$rid" --query status -o tsv)
163	        [[ "$status" != "InProgress" && "$status" != "Queued" ]] && break
164	        sleep 15
165	      done
166	      log "  $p: $status"
167	    done
168	  fi
169	  log "Run phase done. Airflow DAGs fire on their own cron while awake (or trigger in the UI: $AIRFLOW_URL)."
170	}
171	
172	cmd_stop() {
173	  local t j app
174	  log "Putting the harness back to sleep..."
175	  for t in "${TRIGGERS[@]}"; do
176	    az datafactory trigger stop -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --name "$t" >/dev/null && log "  ADF trigger $t -> Stopped"
177	  done
178	  for j in "${JOBS[@]}"; do
179	    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$j/suspend?api-version=$JOB_API" -o none 2>/dev/null && log "  job $j suspended" || true
180	  done
181	  for app in "${APPS_STOP[@]}"; do
182	    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/containerApps/$app/stop?api-version=$APP_API" -o none 2>/dev/null || true
183	    wait_app "$app" Stopped
184	  done
185	  log "Harness is DOWN (burn back to ~CAD 4.5/day, app only)."
186	}
187	
188	cmd=${1:-}; shift || true
189	case "$cmd" in
190	  status) cmd_status ;;
191	  start)  cmd_start ;;
192	  run)    cmd_run "$@" ;;
193	  stop)   cmd_stop ;;
194	  window) cmd_start; cmd_run "$@"; cmd_stop ;;
195	  *) grep '^#' "$0" | sed 's/^# \{0,1\}//' | head -28; exit 1 ;;
196	esac
197

11:# Runs on var.dbt_cron; also startable on demand:  az containerapp job start -n dbt-lineage -g dataq-rg
19:  name                         = "dbt-lineage"
21:  resource_group_name          = data.azurerm_resource_group.dataq.name
29:  schedule_trigger_config {
30:    cron_expression          = var.dbt_cron
37:    username             = azurerm_container_registry.harness.admin_username
38:    password_secret_name = "acr-password"
42:    name  = "acr-password"
46:    name  = "snowflake-password"
50:    name  = "storage-conn"
56:      name    = "dbt-lineage"
64:        name  = "SNOWFLAKE_ACCOUNT"
65:        value = "${var.snowflake_organization_name}-${var.snowflake_account_name}"
68:        name  = "SNOWFLAKE_USER"
72:        name        = "SNOWFLAKE_PASSWORD"
73:        secret_name = "snowflake-password"
76:        name  = "SNOWFLAKE_WAREHOUSE"
80:        name  = "SNOWFLAKE_DATABASE"
85:        name  = "DBT_SNOWFLAKE_ROLE"
90:        name  = "DBT_ARTIFACTS_URI"
91:        value = "adls://${azurerm_storage_account.landing.name}/${var.adls_filesystems[1]}/dbt"
94:        name        = "AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING"
95:        secret_name = "storage-conn"

input.command: az rest --method GET --url "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/37e710fd-b956-4a2e-94f2-b132bb26b3d6/resourceGroups/dataq-rg/providers/Microsoft.App/jobs/dbt-lineage?api-version=2024-08-02-preview" -o json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; p=json.load(sys.stdin)['properties']; print('runningState:', p.get('runningState')); print('cron:', p['configuration']['scheduleTriggerConfig']['cronExpression'])"
input.description: Check dbt-lineage job state and cron

result.output:

input.old_string:
log "  $p: $status"
    done
  fi
  log "Run phase done. Airflow DAGs fire on their own cron while awake (or trigger in the UI: $AIRFLOW_URL)."
}
input.new_string:
log "  $p: $status"
    done
  fi
  if [[ -n "$with_dbt" ]]; then
    log "Starting the $DBT_JOB job (needs live Snowflake!)..."
    az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$DBT_JOB/resume?api-version=$JOB_API" -o none 2>/dev/null || true
    local dexec dstatus
    dexec=$(az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$DBT_JOB/start?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('name',''))")
    log "  $DBT_JOB -> execution ${dexec:-<unknown>}"
    if [[ -n "$dexec" ]]; then
      for _ in $(seq 1 40); do
        dstatus=$(az rest --method GET --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$DBT_JOB/executions/$dexec?api-version=$JOB_API" -o json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['properties']['status'])" 2>/dev/null || echo Unknown)
        [[ "$dstatus" != "Running" && "$dstatus" != "Unknown" ]] && break
        sleep 30
      done
      log "  $DBT_JOB: ${dstatus:-Unknown} (artifacts -> ADLS raw/dbt/latest for the #611 poller)"
    fi
    for _ in $(seq 1 8); do
      az rest --method POST --url "$ARM/Microsoft.App/jobs/$DBT_JOB/suspend?api-version=$JOB_API" -o none 2>/dev/null && break
      sleep 15
    done
    log "  $DBT_JOB re-suspended (nightly cron disarmed)"
  fi
  log "Run phase done. Airflow DAGs fire on their own cron while awake (or trigger in the UI: $AIRFLOW_URL)."
}

input.description: Syntax-check script and show usage header

syntax OK
!/usr/bin/env bash
harness_window.sh — open/close an ad-hoc DataQ harness test window on Azure.

The harness compute was stopped 2026-07-04 (cost wind-down, DataQ #590): while
awake it burns ~CAD 17/day (~0.70/hour); stopped it costs ~0. This script wakes
it, optionally kicks the data flows, and puts it back to sleep.

Usage:
  harness_window.sh status                  # show app/trigger/job states (read-only)
  harness_window.sh start                    # wake: redis -> airflow -> worker -> trigger, ADF triggers on
  harness_window.sh run [--adf] [--dags] [--dbt]
                                             # resume+kick the 5 mockdata jobs as MANUAL executions, re-suspend after
                                             #   --adf:  manual create-run of the Flow-A ADF pipelines*
                                             #   --dbt:  resume+start the dbt-lineage ACA job (#609 — dbt build
                                             #      staging views + mart dynamic tables, artifacts to ADLS), wait,

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import re
p = 'CLAUDE.md'
s = open(p).read()
start = s.index('## 9. Key design decisions (with ADR links)')
end = s.index('## 10. Critical pointers')
new = '''## 9. Key design decisions (ADR index)

The full decision index — one line per ADR with status, 0001–0029 to date — lives at **[docs/adr/README.md](docs/adr/README.md)** and is the **single source of truth** (this section used to duplicate it as a table and the two drifted; it no longer does). Read the index before coding and open the individual ADR whenever a decision bears on your change. The day-to-day operating rules those decisions distill into are already captured in §4–§6, §10 and §11 of this file.

---

'''
s = s[:start] + new + s[end:]
open(p, 'w').write(s)
print("replaced §9,", len(new), "chars in place of", end - start)
EOF
input.description: Replace CLAUDE.md §9 table with index pointer

content.text: Now the docs-consistency-guard agent, whose rule 4 cross-checked the two tables that no longer both exist:

result.output:
48	2. **"Pending" ADRs in `docs/adr/README.md` whose target week has passed.** Today's date is available via `date +%Y-%m-%d`. If an ADR listed under "Pending for Week N" still has no file and the week is past, flag it as overdue.
49	
50	3. **`context/DataQ_platform_roadmap.md` preamble missing or stale.** The preamble should note where the roadmap has been superseded by ADR decisions (ADF is orchestration not a datasource; Airflow was added post-roadmap; DQX deferred to v1.1). If the roadmap is modified without updating the preamble, flag it.
51	
52	4. **ADR status inconsistency.** If CLAUDE.md §9 says an ADR status is "Locked W1" but `docs/adr/README.md` says "Pending", flag the disagreement.
53	
54	5. **Dead working-agreement references.** CLAUDE.md and agent/skill files reference specific working-agreement numbers (e.g., "working-agreement #3", "working-agreement #24"). If `CONTRIBUTING.md` doesn't contain those numbered rules, flag the mismatch.
55	
56	6. **Section numbering drift in CLAUDE.md.** Agent and skill files hard-link to CLAUDE.md sections (e.g., `CLAUDE.md §11`). If sections are renumbered, those external links go stale.
57	
58	7. **Duplicate or drifted rule numbers in CONTRIBUTING.md.** The numbered working agreements must be strictly increasing with no duplicates (this has happened: two rules were numbered 32, fixed in #547). Duplicates make every "working-agreement #N" reference ambiguous; flag them with a renumbering suggestion (and note that renumbering requires updating every doc/agent/skill that cites a rule by number — grep for `rule ?#?N` and `agreement ?#?N` first).
59	
60	8. **`docs/progress.md` headline vs CLAUDE.md §13.** progress.md is the per-PR task ledger; §13 carries only the headline. If progress.md shows a week's tasks complete but §13 still calls that week in-progress (or vice versa), flag it.
61	
62	### 🟢 Acceptable patterns
63	
64	- ADR in "Proposed" status with no file yet — expected before the target week.
65	- Forward ADR numbers reserved in CLAUDE.md §9 as "TBD WN" — placeholders, not dangling refs.
66	- Preamble notes in `context/DataQ_platform_roadmap.md` listing superseding decisions.
67	- CLAUDE.md §13 correctly describing current in-progress work.
68	
69	---

input.old_string: 4. **ADR status inconsistency.** If CLAUDE.md §9 says an ADR status is "Locked W1" but `docs/adr/README.md` says "Pending", flag the disagreement.
input.new_string: 4. **ADR status inconsistency.** `docs/adr/README.md` is the **single** ADR index — CLAUDE.md §9 only links to it. For every ADR file, the index must have a row and its status must agree with the ADR's own `Status:` line; flag missing rows and disagreements. Also flag any re-grown decision table in CLAUDE.md §9 (it was deliberately reduced to a pointer — duplication is the drift vector).

input.command:
git add CLAUDE.md docs/adr/README.md .claude/agents/docs-consistency-guard.md && git commit -m "docs(adr): single-source the ADR index — CLAUDE.md §9 links docs/adr/README.md instead of duplicating it

The §9 decision table duplicated the docs/adr/README.md index and the two
drifted (README still carried ADR 0026 as Proposed/Pending while the ADR
file and §9 say Accepted, phase 1 built 2026-07-04). Single-source it:

- CLAUDE.md §9 is now a pointer to the index (the distilled operating
  rules stay in §4–§6/§10–§11); ~9KB of per-session context dropped
- docs/adr/README.md: 0026 moved from Pending into the main index as
  Accepted (matches the ADR's own Status line); Pending keeps only 0015
- docs-consistency-guard rule 4 retargeted: index rows must agree with
  each ADR file's Status line (and flag any re-grown §9 table)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push -u origin docs/adr-index-single-source
input.description: Commit and push ADR index single-sourcing
